content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains six space-separated integers R, C, sr, sc, tr, and tc.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of different cells that would be visited when the cell (tr, tc) is found.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The algorithm traverses the grid in a specific order (right, down, left, up) and counts the number of cells visited before reaching the target cell.
The algorithm visits all cells in the grid before reaching the target cell.
The algorithm starts and ends at the same cell, so only one cell is visited.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by analyzing the order in which the cells are visited. The algorithm starts at cell (sr, sc) and visits the unvisited adjacent cells inside the grid recursively. The order in which the adjacent cells are looked for are: right, down, left, and up. We can calculate the number of visited cells before reaching the target cell (tr, tc) by considering the number of rows and columns traversed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no additional space is used.
